sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, value1 FLOAT, value2 FLOAT, str_value VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value1, value2, str_value) VALUES (1, 10.5, NULL, 'A'), (2, NULL, 5.5, 'B'), (3, 3.5, NULL, NULL), (4, 7.5, 7.5, 'C'), (5, 0, 0.0, 'D');
CREATE TABLE second_table (id INT, related_id INT, num_value INT);
INSERT INTO second_table (id, related_id, num_value) VALUES (1, 1, 4), (2, 2, 8), (3, 3, NULL), (4, NULL, 10);
SELECT t.id, SUM(t.value1) + (91 & 15) AS total FROM test_table t GROUP BY t.id HAVING total > 0;
SELECT t.id, SUM((t.value1 + t.value2) * (CASE WHEN str_value IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE 1 END)) AS computed_sum FROM test_table t WHERE t.value1 IS NOT NULL OR t.value2 IS NOT NULL GROUP BY t.id;
SELECT t1.id, t1.value1, SUM(t2.num_value) AS related_sum FROM test_table t1 LEFT JOIN second_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.related_id GROUP BY t1.id HAVING (SUM(t2.num_value) & 10) = 0;
SELECT DISTINCT t.id, SUM(COALESCE(t.value1, 0) + (t.value2 & 15)) AS sum_result FROM test_table t GROUP BY t.id ORDER BY sum_result DESC;
SELECT t.id, SUM(CAST(t.str_value AS VARCHAR)) AS sum_str FROM test_table t WHERE str_value SIMILAR TO '[A-Z]' GROUP BY t.id HAVING (SUM(COALESCE(t.value1, 0)) & 7) > 3;
SELECT t.id, SUM(t.value1) AS sum1, SUM(NULLIF(t.value2, 0)) AS sum2 FROM test_table t GROUP BY t.id HAVING (sum1 + (91 & sum2)) > 10;
SELECT (SELECT AVG(value1) FROM test_table) AS avg_value, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM second_table WHERE num_value IS NOT NULL) AS non_null_count;
